description_long: This object consists of three triangular rice balls, known as onigiri, neatly arranged side by side on a rectangular wooden tray. Each rice ball is formed from white rice and wrapped partially with a rectangular strip of dark green nori seaweed, covering the base and extending up the front. The rice texture is detailed, with individual grains visible, giving a realistic and appetizing appearance. The wooden tray is smooth, flat, and has a natural wood grain pattern, providing a clean and traditional presentation. There are no visible garnishes, sauces, or additional toppings on the rice balls. The overall presentation is simple, clean, and typical of Japanese cuisine served in casual or urban settings.
description: Three triangular onigiri rice balls with nori seaweed wraps are arranged on a wooden tray, with visible rice texture and no additional garnishes.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three onigiri rice balls with their nori seaweed wraps facing forward on a wooden tray.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thickness of the rice balls and the edge of the nori wrap, with the wooden tray visible beneath.
description_view_2: Rear angled view similar to the front, showing the backs of the onigiri and the full length of the wooden tray.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, mirroring view 1, showing the side profile of the rice balls and the nori wrap edges.
